questions.question_text: During which period must WV real estate licenses be renewed annually?
questions.explanation: WV licenses must be renewed annually between May 1 and June 30. Late renewal is available July 1 through August 31 with a $50 late fee.

questions.question_text: A licensee misses the June 30 renewal deadline. What additional fee must they pay if they renew by August 31?
questions.explanation: Late renewal between July 1 and August 31 incurs a $50 late fee, bringing the total renewal cost to $125. Additional reinstatement options with fees exist through December 31, after which the license is canceled.

questions.question_text: WV license renewals are processed through which system?
questions.explanation: License renewals are completed through the WVREC's Online License Renewal System. This is separate from the initial application system.

questions.question_text: How often must WV real estate licenses be renewed?
questions.explanation: WV requires annual license renewal between May 1 and June 30. This is more frequent than many states that use biennial (every 2 years) renewal cycles.

questions.question_text: A salesperson fails to renew by December 31. What happens to their license?
questions.explanation: If a licensee fails to renew by December 31, the license is canceled and they must reapply from scratch. Regular renewal is May 1-June 30, late renewal with a $50 fee is July 1-August 31, and additional reinstatement options exist through December 31.

questions.question_text: A WV licensee is no longer associated with a broker. What is their license status?
questions.explanation: An inactive license means the licensee is not currently associated with a broker and cannot practice. An active license requires broker association.

questions.question_text: Which license status allows a WV licensee to actively practice real estate?
questions.explanation: Only an active license allows practice. Active status requires both a current license and association with an employing broker.

questions.question_text: What is the difference between a 'canceled' and 'expired' license in West Virginia?
questions.correct_answer: Canceled is missed renewal; expired has lapsed further
distractors: Both terms mean the same license status
Canceled is voluntary; expired is involuntary
Expired allows renewal; canceled does not allow it
questions.explanation: A canceled license was not renewed by the deadline. An expired license has lapsed beyond the renewal grace period. Both prevent the licensee from practicing.

questions.question_text: A salesperson on inactive status receives a request to show property. Can they legally do so?
distractors: Yes, with the listing broker's permission
Yes, as long as they don't negotiate
questions.explanation: An inactive license means the licensee cannot practice real estate in any capacity, including showing property. They must associate with a broker and activate their license first.

questions.question_text: Which of the following is NOT a recognized WV license status?
questions.explanation: WV recognizes four license statuses: active, inactive, canceled, and expired. 'Provisional' is not a valid WV license status.

questions.question_text: How many hours of continuing education must WV licensees complete per fiscal year?
questions.explanation: WV requires 7 hours of continuing professional education per fiscal year in commission-approved courses. This is on the lower end compared to many states.

questions.question_text: A licensee takes a CE course on contract law this year. Can they repeat the same course next year for CE credit?
questions.correct_answer: No, cannot repeat for 2 consecutive periods
distractors: Yes, any approved course counts each year
No, courses can never be repeated
questions.explanation: A licensee may not repeat the same course (same title and content) for two consecutive renewal periods (2 years). They can retake it after a one-year gap.

questions.question_text: A new licensee passed both exam sections in May and their first renewal is due June 30 of the same fiscal year. Must they complete CE?
questions.explanation: Licensees who passed both exam sections during the fiscal year of their first required renewal are exempt from CE for that initial period if they renew by June 30.

questions.question_text: Who must provide continuing education courses for WV licensees?
distractors: The WVREC directly, no outside providers
Any licensed broker with 10+ years experience
questions.explanation: CE courses must be offered by commission-approved providers and taught by commission-approved instructors. Not just any educational institution qualifies.

questions.question_text: Starting FY2024, brokers and associate brokers must include how many broker-level CE hours within their 7-hour requirement?
questions.explanation: Beginning FY2024, brokers and associate brokers must include 3 hours of broker-level continuing education within their total 7-hour annual CE requirement.

questions.question_text: When a WV salesperson changes employing brokers, who must notify the WVREC?
distractors: Both the salesperson and departing broker
questions.explanation: When a salesperson changes brokers, the transfer must be reported to WVREC, and the new broker must notify the commission of the association.

questions.question_text: A salesperson leaves Broker A and joins Broker B. What must happen with WVREC?
questions.correct_answer: The transfer must be reported to WVREC
distractors: The salesperson must retake the exam
questions.explanation: When a salesperson changes brokers, the transfer must be reported to the WVREC. The new broker must notify the commission of the new association.

questions.question_text: During the period between leaving one broker and joining another, what is the salesperson's license status?
questions.explanation: A WV salesperson must be associated with a broker to hold an active license. Between brokers, the license is inactive and the salesperson cannot practice.

questions.question_text: Can a WV salesperson be associated with more than one employing broker simultaneously?
questions.correct_answer: No, one employing broker at a time
distractors: Yes, up to two brokers allowed
Yes, if both brokers consent in writing
questions.explanation: A WV salesperson must be associated with one employing broker. When changing brokers, the transfer must be reported to the WVREC and the new broker notifies the commission.

questions.question_text: A salesperson transfers from one brokerage to another. Must they complete additional education to effect the transfer?
questions.correct_answer: No, just report the transfer to WVREC
distractors: Yes, must retake the state exam portion
questions.explanation: Transferring between brokers requires only that the change be reported to the WVREC. No additional education or examination is needed for a license transfer.
